title: [MooreToCore] LSB and MSB support in moore dialect
body:
Hi! I tried using `circt-verilog` and ran into problems.

```verilog
module Mod(input clk, input [4:3] a, output logic b);
always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    b <= a[3];
end
endmodule
```

```verilog
module Mod(input [1:0] a, output logic b);
always @(posedge a)
    b <= a[0];
endmodule
```

In first case lowering fro Moore to Core dialect fails due to there are no information about range (`[4:3]`) in Moore dialect and out-of-range error occurs. Second case isn't implemented now (see corresponding [todo](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/dbd1d7c246627e3edc3bcc757cb38e846d7985d2/lib/Conversion/MooreToCore/MooreToCore.cpp#L433)). According to 9.4.2 IEEE 1800-2017, _an edge event shall be detected only on the LSB of the expression_. For this reason LSB information is needed for this todo.

I would help CIRCT project and implement support for these cases. Is it good idea to add LSB and MSB as attribute? Are there other possible solutions to this problem?

Thank you in advance!
